consequences: Future sessions should not conflate publish-readiness with exploratory model development. The current draft can move toward publication through the publish-track tasks, while research-track tasks remain available to deepen or extend the argument without blocking publication.
context: The previous board grouped the current work into broad editorial/evidence/repro tasks plus a single low-priority legacy-repair task. Review of the draft and model package shows distinct needs: immediate coherence and validity work for publication, and separate simulation/model-development opportunities for future strengthening.
decision: Treat tasks 007-009 plus task-015 as the canonical publish track. Treat task-010 through task-014 as separate research-track work for validity strengthening and future simulations, especially fragility robustness, ABM redesign, SFC redesign, and an inflation/resource-boundary model.
title: Money-Minsky board split into publish-track and research-track lanes
